topic: Holistic verification of EXP-452 integration (P2: detail_group on 11 SC cluster WINs; P3: WIN-068 finding cleanup; P4: WIN-058 finding rewrite; P6: b-suffix convention note)
our_argument_summary: EXP-452 ships eight integration patches in response to HOL-TAXONOMY-c2; four (P2/P3/P4/P6) are data-side and now live in data/wins.json + data/sections.json (decider commit e0b57e1, 2026-05-30T14:49Z). P2 populates detail_group on 11 Self-Contradicted WINs across three clusters — Schumann (WIN-002/029/038/061), cosmological-bounded-geometry (WIN-047/048/052/054), and H(r)/coupling-law (WIN-044/056/067) — and reassigns WIN-061 out of the magnetic-broad cluster into Schumann on the grounds that its finding leads with the cavity-22 Hz argument. P3 strips the WIN-068 duplication sentence from the visible finding one-liner (the duplication concern is preserved in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict). P4 rewrites the WIN-058 finding one-liner to lead with the V12/V13 reconciliation failure, surfacing the Misleading-qualifying structural problem instead of the non-discrimination framing. P6 adds a one-paragraph WIN-numbering note explaining the b-suffix convention to Part 3.1 of sections.json, immediately ahead of the verdict tally.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new code paths; data-only integration. P2 writes detail_group on 11 WIN records. P3 modifies WIN-068.finding string. P4 modifies WIN-058.finding string. P6 inserts an HTML paragraph in sections.json.part3.html. Build pipeline re-runs generate-html.js on next publish to surface the changes in docs/index.html — already done in this commit (5-line index.html delta from this run).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the analyst surfaced WIN-061 reassignment as a curmudgeon-attention boundary call (operations[3].note) and the reassignment is the only judgment call in the patch set.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Holistic taxonomy verification — no per-WIN dome code analysis applies. The cluster-tagging patches do not alter any monitoring/relabels/post_hoc/derives_from_dome judgments on the affected WINs.

holes_found.description: Magnetic-broad cluster label is now stale on the 8 remaining members. WIN-040, 041, 042, 043, 057b, 059, 060, and 063 all still carry detail_group="WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063", which by the inclusive-range convention claims WIN-061 as a member. But P2 just moved WIN-061 out of magnetic-broad and into Schumann (its detail_group is now "WIN-002/029/038/061"). The same WIN-061 is therefore claimed by two cluster labels: the Schumann tag on its own row says it lives in Schumann, while the magnetic-broad tag on eight sibling rows still says it lives in magnetic-broad. A careful reader cross-referencing the two surfaces will catch this. The label also continues to omit WIN-057b, which was already a magnetic-broad member before this patch but was never written into the label.
holes_found.recommendation: Update detail_group on the 8 magnetic-broad members from "WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063" to a label that accurately reflects current membership and drops 061 — e.g. "WIN-040-043, 057b, 059-060, 063" (8 members: 040, 041, 042, 043, 057b, 059, 060, 063). Same value across all 8 rows.

holes_found.description: Three detail_evidence prose blocks on WIN-059, WIN-060, and WIN-063 hardcode the stale cluster string "WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063" inline (e.g. WIN-063: "This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063 and reuses NOAA WMM data already cited in WIN-004/005"). These are exactly the same label as the detail_group field, but embedded in the rendered prose where readers see it explicitly. After P2, these references both (a) include WIN-061, which has been reassigned out, and (b) omit WIN-057b, which is a member. Because docs/index.html is regenerated from sections.json + wins.json by build-scripts/generate-html.js, the live site already echoes the stale string in the WIN-059/060/063 panels (the e0b57e1 commit included a 5-line index.html delta that did not rewrite these prose blocks).
holes_found.recommendation: Update the three detail_evidence strings on WIN-059, WIN-060, WIN-063 to use the corrected cluster label from Hole 1, then rebuild docs/index.html. Same fix can be a single coordinated patch addressing both Hole 1 and Hole 2.

holes_found.description: P1 (the four-cluster symmetric-counting acknowledgement paragraph in the Evaluation Guide) is still pending operator-apply (ISS-2341, generate-html.js). That means the data layer now carries four cluster tags (tidal already in place; Schumann, cosmological, H(r) added today via P2) while the visible prose layer in the Verdict Assignment Criteria block still acknowledges only the tidal cluster. The mismatch is transient by design — P1 will close it — but until P1 lands, a reader who clicks through to a Schumann/cosmological/H(r) WIN and sees the new detail_group tag has no companion paragraph explaining what the cluster means or why we count this way. The risk window is whatever interval separates P2-P4-P6 deploy from the operator-applied P1 prose deploy.
holes_found.recommendation: Track this as a deploy-ordering concern, not a separate patchable hole. When the operator applies P1, the gap closes automatically. If P1 is going to wait more than a few days, consider gating P2 visibility on the front-end (omit detail_group rendering until P1 lands) or land a minimal interim acknowledgement paragraph. No action required if P1 will be applied promptly.

holes_found.description: Part 1b sections.json ("New Content Breakdown") classifies WIN-059, WIN-060, and WIN-061 together as "Re-sliced geomagnetic data (WIN-040 through WIN-043, WIN-053, WIN-059-061, WIN-063): 9 WINs". After P2 retaxonomizes WIN-061 as Schumann (primary structural failure: cavity 22 Hz, not INTERMAGNET re-slicing), this narration is in tension with our own cluster taxonomy. The defense is that part1b describes the dome's V51.0 release grouping rather than our cluster taxonomy, but a careful reader who reaches Part 3 and sees WIN-061 tagged Schumann will reasonably ask why Part 1b counted it as magnetic. This existed before P2 (the description was inherited from the dome's own bundling) but is now sharper.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: when P1 lands, add a one-line gloss to part1b acknowledging that one of the three magnetic-bundled WINs (061) is taxonomically Schumann in our cluster classification, while leaving the count of "9 WINs" as the dome's own grouping. Or simply move WIN-061 from the magnetic line to a new Schumann line in the dome-grouping breakdown.

stronger_arguments.description: The WIN-061 reassignment from magnetic-broad to Schumann is defensible because WIN-061.finding leads with "Dome cavity predicts ~22 Hz base frequency; suppression pattern follows ionospheric (globe) physics" — the primary-issue rule (generate-html.js:1764) directly supports clustering by primary structural failure. A defender who cried "you moved WIN-061 mid-stream to inflate the Schumann cluster from 3 to 4" can be answered by pointing to WIN-061.finding and noting that the prior magnetic-broad assignment used the dome's own V51.0 bundle as a placeholder (descriptive of dome behavior, not our cluster taxonomy). The patch could be marginally stronger by saying this out loud in the WIN-061 detail_evidence or in the P1 paragraph itself — a one-sentence audit trail along the lines of "WIN-061 is bundled with magnetic WINs in the dome's V51.0 release but its primary structural failure is the Schumann cavity floor, so we cluster it with WIN-002/029/038." Without that, the move looks asserted; with it, it looks reasoned.

kernel_of_truth.description: The P2/P3/P4/P6 integrations are correct in shape: P2 instantiates the cluster taxonomy that P1 will narrate (data/wins.json detail_group on 11 WINs, all preserving their existing Self-Contradicted verdicts and claim text); P3 strips the duplication-in-finding sentence on WIN-068 that the c2 review flagged, while preserving the substantive content in detail_verdict_text ("WIN-010 (BOU 2017), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), and WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble 1991–2016) all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels. The dome counts each as a separate confirmed prediction, inflating the score count without adding independent evidence." — data/wins.json:WIN-068.detail_verdict_text) and the TLDR ("Triple-counting WIN-010/025/068 for one mechanism inflates the score." — data/wins.json:WIN-068.tldr_verdict); P4 produces a WIN-058 finding one-liner that genuinely leads with the V12/V13 reconciliation failure ("V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so the V13 angular identity coexists with the V12 distance-scaling factor without reconciliation; the coordinate system also requires WGS-84 longitude as input (OPEN-001 unresolved) and achieves 7.3% error where Vincenty geodesy achieves sub-0.01% on the same WGS-84 inputs." — data/wins.json:WIN-058.finding); P6 places the b-suffix convention note exactly where the EXP specified, immediately after the "Click any WIN number" line and ahead of the Verdict Tally (data/sections.json:part3.html). No verdict drift: spot-checked all 11 P2-tagged WINs (002, 029, 038, 044, 047, 048, 052, 054, 056, 061, 067) and they all retain Self-Contradicted; WIN-068 retains Std Model Explains; WIN-058 retains Misleading. The boundary call on WIN-061 is defensible per the analyst's note and per WIN-061's own finding leading with the cavity 22 Hz argument (data/wins.json:WIN-061.finding).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The integration is correct in shape but incomplete in scope: when P2 moved WIN-061 out of the magnetic-broad cluster, it did not update the magnetic-broad cluster label on the 8 remaining members (040, 041, 042, 043, 057b, 059, 060, 063). The label still reads "WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063" — naming WIN-061 as a member it no longer covers, and omitting WIN-057b which it does cover. The same stale label is also embedded in three detail_evidence prose strings (WIN-059, WIN-060, WIN-063 — data/wins.json:1310/1331/1394). After build (docs/index.html), readers see two surfaces disagreeing about where WIN-061 lives. The EXP's own operations[3].note flagged this exact reassignment for curmudgeon attention but the patch set did not include the symmetric cleanup of the residual cluster, which is the consistency hole. This is the kind of "you criticize me for X then do X" rhetorical opening that the c2 review (HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-Hole-1, EXP-452-HOL-TAXONOMY-c2-application-consistency.json:hole_to_patch_mapping) explicitly framed as the strongest dome-defender process objection. Closing 7/8 of the cluster's cross-references and leaving 11 (eight detail_group fields + three prose strings) misaligned is a partial close.

deeper_analysis: The four-cluster taxonomy is the project's answer to the c2 "counting hypocrisy" objection — we acknowledge that the 5 tidal WINs (045/046/049/050/051), 4 Schumann WINs (002/029/038/061), 4 cosmological WINs (047/048/052/054), and 3 H(r)/coupling WINs (044/056/067) each derive from a single underlying geometric impossibility, so a defender can fairly say the 16 SC-clustered WINs are at most 4 independent logical units. P1 (pending operator-apply) ships this acknowledgement in the Evaluation Guide. P2 (live as of e0b57e1) instantiates the cluster taxonomy in the data layer by populating detail_group. The patch sequencing is sound: instantiate the tags first, then publish the prose that explains them. The risk of the current state — P2 live, P1 pending — is bounded: a reader who reaches the Schumann/cosmological/H(r) WINs before P1 lands sees the new detail_group label without context, but the cluster names are self-descriptive ("WIN-002/029/038/061" reads as a four-member group) and the worst case is a temporary opacity rather than a misstatement. The harder issue is the residual magnetic-broad cluster: removing WIN-061 from it without rewriting the label leaves the label internally inconsistent in a way a defender can use immediately (see Hole 1 + Hole 2). The fix is small (one find/replace across 8 detail_group fields + 3 detail_evidence prose strings, plus a rebuild) and should land before P1 — P1 will draw attention to cluster bookkeeping, so any inconsistency in cluster bookkeeping at that moment is a visible target.

citations_verified: data/sections.json:part3.html — b-suffix convention note present after "Click any WIN number" line
git commit e0b57e1 — decider EXP-452 P2/P3/P4/P6 integration commit
citations_unchecked: P1/P5/P7/P8 (generate-html.js) — pending operator-apply (ISS-2341, ISS-2342); not in scope for this review

summary_for_decider: EXP-452 P2/P3/P4/P6 integration is correct in shape — no verdict drift, WIN-061 boundary call is defensible per the primary-issue rule, finding rewrites land cleanly, b-suffix note is in the right place. Two moderate-severity holes: the magnetic-broad cluster label still includes WIN-061 (it should not) and still omits WIN-057b (it should not), both in the detail_group field on 8 WINs and in the inline prose string on WIN-059/060/063. A single coordinated find/replace patch can fix both ("WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063" → "WIN-040-043, 057b, 059-060, 063"). Two minor flags: P1 still pending operator-apply (transient narrative-vs-data gap), and part1b's magnetic-content breakdown still lists WIN-061 (descriptive of dome bundling, in tension with our cluster taxonomy after this patch). Recommend: minor_edit patch addressing Holes 1 + 2 before P1 ships, since P1 will draw attention to cluster bookkeeping.
